ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, DEFENDANT ALBAY CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT ALBAY CONSTRUCTION CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SUSTAINED ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION AND PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS (INSULATION) ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO MR. AY'S DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AN ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-08-2010 Off Calendar

Defendant Crane Co.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-05-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
James P. Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL, appearing for plaintiff and opposing party Kenneth Roush
Megan Cesare-Eastman, Esq. of K&L GATES LLP, appearing for defendant and moving party Crane Co.

RULING:
Defendant Crane Co.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether his removal of packing from one or two valves attributable to defendant is a substantial factor in causing his asbestos-related illness. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Lavena Ward CSR# 7077 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-04-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment is continued on the court's own motion to November 5, 2010 at 10:00 a.m. in Department 220. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Oct-22-2010 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Nov-04-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jasun C. Molinelli, Esq. of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for defendant / moving party Albay Construction Company
Gilbert L. Purcell, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Kenneth Roush

RULING:
DEFENDANT ALBAY CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Albay Construction Co.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (insulation) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to Mr. Ay's declaration are sustained. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
